questions.questionText: Let the vertex A of a triangle ABC be $(1,2)$, and the mid-point of the side AB be $(5,-1)$. If the centroid of this triangle is $(3,4)$ and its circumcenter is $(\alpha, \beta)$, then $21(\alpha+\beta)$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The number of functions $f:\{1,2,3,4\} \rightarrow\{a, b, c\}$, which are not onto, is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The area of the region $\{(x, y): y \leq \pi-|x|, y \leq|x \sin x|, y \geq 0\}$ is:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $y=y(x)$ be the solution of the differential equation $\frac{d y}{d x}=\left(1+x+x^2\right)\left(1-y+y^2\right), y(0)=\frac{1}{2}$. Then $(2 y(1)-1)$ is equal to
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If the set of all solutions of $\left|x^2+x-9\right|=|x|+\left|x^2-9\right|$ is $[\alpha, \beta] \cup[\gamma, \infty)$, then ( $\alpha^2+\beta^2+\gamma^2$ ) is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $\mathrm{S}=\left\{\mathrm{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ll}a & b \\ c & d\end{array}\right]: a, b, c, d \in\{0,1,2,3,4\}\right.$ and $\left.\mathrm{A}^2-4 \mathrm{~A}+3 \mathrm{I}=0\right\}$ be a set of $2 \times 2$ matrices. Then the number of matrices in S , for which the sum of the diagonal elements is equal to 4 , is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The first term of an A.P. of 30 non-negative terms is $\frac{10}{3}$. If the sum of this A.P. is the cube of its last term, then its common difference is:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let the line $\mathrm{L}_1: x+3=0$ intersect the lines $\mathrm{L}_2: x-y=0$ and $\mathrm{L}_3: 3 x+y=0$ at the points A and B , respectively. Let the bisector of the obtuse angle between the lines $L_2$ and $L_3$ intersect the line $L_1$ at the point $C$. Then $B C^2: A C^2$ is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let the smallest value of $k \in \mathbb{N}$, for which the coefficient of $x^3$ in $(1+x)^3+(1+x)^4+(1+x)^5+\ldots+(1+x)^{99}+(1+k x)^{100}, x \neq 0$, is $\left(43 n+\frac{101}{4}\right)\left({ }^{100} \mathrm{C}_3\right)$ for some $n \in \mathrm{~N}$, be $p$. Then the value of $p+n$ is :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 1 & 2 \\ -2 & 0 & 1 \\ 1 & 3 & 5\end{array}\right]$. Then the sum of all elements of the matrix $\operatorname{adj}\left(\operatorname{adj}\left(2(\operatorname{adj} \mathrm{~A})^{-1}\right)\right)$ is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $\int\limits_{-2}^2(|\sin x|+[x \sin x]) d x=2(3-\cos 2)+\beta$, where [ ⋅ ] is the greatest integer function. Then $\beta \sin \left(\frac{\beta}{2}\right)$ equals:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Suppose that the mean and median of the non-negative numbers $21,8,17, a, 51,103, b, 13,67,(a>b)$, are 40 and 21 , respectively. If the mean deviation about the median is 26 , then $2 a$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
Let [•] denote the greatest integer function. If the domain of the function

$f(x)=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{4 x+2[x]}{3}\right)$ is $[\alpha, \beta]$, then $12(\alpha+\beta)$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The square of the distance of the point $(-2,-8,6)$ from the line $\frac{x-1}{1}=\frac{y-1}{2}=\frac{z}{-1}$ along the line $\frac{x+5}{1}=\frac{y+5}{-1}=\frac{z}{2}$ is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: If $y=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 \cos x-4 \sin x}{4 \cos x+3 \sin x}\right)+2 \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{1+\sqrt{1-x^2}}\right)$, then $\frac{d y}{d x}$ at $x=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: The number of ways, of forming a queue of 4 boys and 3 girls such that all the girls are not together, is:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: A line with direction ratios $1,-1,2$ intersects the lines $\frac{x}{2}=\frac{y}{3}=\frac{z+1}{3}$ and $\frac{x+1}{-1}=\frac{y-2}{1}=\frac{z}{4}$ at the points P and Q , respectively. If the length of the line segment PQ is $\alpha$, then $225 \alpha^2$ is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Suppose that two chords, drawn from the point $(1,2)$ on the circle $x^2+y^2+x-3 y=0$ are bisected by the $y$-axis. If the other ends of these chords are R and S , and the mid point of the line segment RS is $(\alpha, \beta)$, then $6(\alpha+\beta)$ is equal to :
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $f$ be a real polynomial of degree $n$ such that $f(x)=f^{\prime}(x) f^{\prime \prime}(x)$, for all $x \in \mathbb{R}$. If $f(0)=0$, then $36\left(f^{\prime}(2)+f^{\prime \prime}(2)+\int_0^2 f(x) d x\right)$ is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $z$ be a complex number such that $|z+2|=|z-2|$ and arg $\left(\frac{z+3}{z-i}\right)=\frac{\pi}{4}$. Then $|z|^2$ is equal to:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Let $\overrightarrow{a_k}=\left(\tan \theta_k\right) \hat{i}+\hat{j}$ and $\overrightarrow{b_k}=\hat{i}-\left(\cot \theta_k\right) \hat{j}$, where $\theta_k=\frac{2^{k-1} \pi}{2^n+1}$, for some $n \in \mathbb{N}, n>5$. Then the value of $\frac{\sum\limits_{k=1}^n\left|\overrightarrow{a_k}\right|^2}{\sum\limits_{k=1}^n\left|\overrightarrow{b_k}\right|^2}$ is
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
If $\mathrm{A}=\frac{\sin 3^{\circ}}{\cos 9^{\circ}}+\frac{\sin 9^{\circ}}{\cos 27^{\circ}}+\frac{\sin 27^{\circ}}{\cos 81^{\circ}}$ and $\mathrm{B}=\tan 81^{\circ}-\tan 3^{\circ}$, then $\frac{\mathrm{B}}{\mathrm{A}}$ is equal to

$\_\_\_\_$ .
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: A coin is tossed 8 times. If the probability that exactly 4 heads appear in the first six tosses and exactly 3 heads appear in the last five tosses is $p$, then $96 p$ is equal to $\_\_\_\_$ .
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Consider the parabola $\mathrm{P}: y^2=4 k x$ and the ellipse $\mathrm{E}: \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1$. Let the line segment joining the points of intersection of P and E , be their latus rectums. If the eccentricity of E is $e$, then $e^2+2 \sqrt{2}$ is equal to $\_\_\_\_$ .
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText: Two 4 bits binary numbers, $A=1101$ and $B=1010$ are given in the inputs of a logic circuit shown in figure below. The output $(Y)$ will be :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
An ideal gas undergoes a process maintaining relation between pressure $(P)$ and $\operatorname{volume}(V)$ as $P=P_{\mathrm{o}}\left(1+\left(\frac{V_{\mathrm{o}}}{V}\right)^2\right)^{-1}$, where $P_{\mathrm{o}}$ and $V_{\mathrm{o}}$ are constants. If two samples $A$ and $B$ (two moles each) with initial volumes $V_{\mathrm{o}}$ and $3 V_{\mathrm{o}}$ respectively undergo above mentioned process and attain same pressure, then the difference at the temperatures of these samples, $T_B-T_A$ is $\_\_\_\_$ .

( $R=$ gas constant)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
The increase in the pressure required to decrease the volume ( $\Delta V$ ) of water is $6.3 \times 10^7 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2$. The percentage decrease in the volume is $\_\_\_\_$ .

(Bulk modulus of water $=2.1 \times 10^9 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2$.)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
The number of points, at which the function $f(x)=\max \left\{6 x, 2+3 x^2\right\}+|x-1| \cos \left|x^2-\frac{1}{4}\right|, x \in(-\pi, \pi)$, is not differentiable, is

$\_\_\_\_$ .
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.questionText:
A string $A$ of length 0.314 m and Young's modulus $2 \times 10^{10} \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2$ is connected to another string $B$ of length and Young's modulus both twice of those of $A$. This series combination of strings is then suspended from a rigid support and its free end is fixed to a load of mass 0.8 kg . The net change in length of the combination is $\_\_\_\_$ mm.

(radius of both the strings is 0.2 mm and acceleration due to gravity $=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ ) (Mass of both strings is to be neglected as compared to the mass of load)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A voltmeter with internal resistance of $x \Omega$ can be used to measure upto 20 V . In order to increase its measuring range to 30 V , the required modification is to $\_\_\_\_$ .
options:
(A)

connect resistor of $\frac{x}{2} \Omega$, in series with voltmeter.

(B)

connect resistor of $\frac{x}{2} \Omega$, in parallel to voltmeter.

(C)

connect a resistor of $x \Omega$ in series with voltmeter.

(D)

connect resistor of $2 x \Omega$ in parallel to voltmeter.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A rod of length 10 cm lies along the principle axis of a concave mirror of focal length 10 cm as shown in figure. The length of the image is $\_\_\_\_$ cm.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In a screw gauge when the circular scale is given five complete rotations it moves linearly by 2.5 mm . If the circular scale has 100 divisions, the least count of screw gauge is $\_\_\_\_$ mm.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: An insulated wire is wound so that it forms a flat coil with $N=200$ turns. The radius of the innermost turn is $r_1=3 \mathrm{~cm}$, and of the outermost turn $r_2=6 \mathrm{~cm}$. If 20 mA current flows in it then the magnetic moment will be $\alpha \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~A} . \mathrm{m}^2$. The value of $\alpha$ is $\_\_\_\_$ .
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The graph shows variation of stopping potential $V_{\mathrm{o}}$ with the frequency $v$ of the incident radiation for three photosensitive metals $X_1, X_2$ and $X_3$. Which metal will give out electrons with greater kinetic energy, for the same wavelength of incident radiation?
options:
(A)

$X_1$

(B)

$X_2$

(C)

$X_3$

(D) All the metals will give out photo electrons with same kinetic energies.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: An unpolarized light is incident on the plane interface of air-dielectric medium shown in figure. If the incident angle is equal to Brewster angle, identify the expression representing reflected wave.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
A telescope with objective diameter $R$ is used to observe a distant star emitting light of wavelength 500 nm , at a resolution of $5 \times 10^{-7}$ radian. The value of $R$ is

$\_\_\_\_$ cm .
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The voltage and the current between $A$ and $B$ points shown in the circuit are $\_\_\_\_$ .
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A solid sphere of mass $M$ and radius $R$ is divided into two unequal parts. The smaller part having mass $M / 8$ is converted into a sphere of radius $r$ and the larger part is converted into a circular disc of thickness $t$ and radius $2 R$. If $I_1$ is moment of inertia of a sphere having radius $r$ about an axis through its centre and $I_2$ is the moment of inertia of a disc about its diameter, the ratio of their moment of inertia $I_2 / I_1=$ $\_\_\_\_$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A parallel plate air capacitor is connected to a battery. The plates are pulled apart at uniform speed $v$. If $x$ is the separation between the plates at any instant, then the time rate of change of electrostatic energy of the capacitor is proportional to $x^\alpha$, where $\alpha$ is $\_\_\_\_$ .
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A slit of width $a$ is illuminated by light of wavelength $\lambda$. The linear separation between $1^{\text {st }}$ and $3^{\text {rd }}$ minima in the diffraction pattern produced on a screen placed at a distance $D$ from the slit system is $\_\_\_\_$ .
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The time taken by a block of mass $m$ to slide down from the highest point to the lowest point on a rough inclined plane is $50 \%$ more compared to the time taken by the same block on identical inclined smooth plane. Both inclined planes are at $45^{\circ}$ with the horizontal. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the rough inclined surface and block is $\_\_\_\_$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Consider a circuit consisting of a capacitor $(20 \mu \mathrm{~F})$, resistor $(100 \Omega)$ and two identical diodes as shown in figure. The resistance of diode under forward biasing condition is $10 \Omega$. The time constant of the circuit is $\alpha \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~s}$. The value of $\alpha$ is $\_\_\_\_$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: One gas of $n_1$ mole of molecules at temperature $T_1$, volume $V_1$, and pressure $P_1$, and another gas of $n_2$ mole of molecules at temperature $T_2$, volume $V_2$, and pressure $P_2$, are mixed resulting in pressure $P$ and volume $V$ of the mixture. The temperature of the mixture is $\_\_\_\_$ .
options: (C) $\frac{\left(T_2 P_1 V_1+T_1 P_2 V_2\right)}{T_1 T_2 P V}$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two nuclei of mass number 3 combine with another nucleus of mass number 4 to yield a nucleus of mass number 10. If the binding energy per nucleon for the mass numbers 3,4 and 10 are $5.6 \mathrm{MeV}, 7.4 \mathrm{MeV}$ and 6.1 MeV , respectively, then in the process, $\Delta \mathrm{Mc}^2=$ $\_\_\_\_$ MeV .
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: An inductor of 10 mH , capacitor of $0.1 \mu \mathrm{~F}$ and a resistor of $100 \Omega$ are connected in series across an $a . c$ power supply $220 \mathrm{~V}, 70 \mathrm{~Hz}$. The power factor of the given circuit is 0.5 . The difference in the inductive reactance and capacitance reactance is $\sqrt{3} \alpha \Omega$. The value of $\alpha$ is $\_\_\_\_$ .
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A body of mass 2 kg begins to move under the influence of time dependent force $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{F}}=\left(2 \mathrm{t} \hat{\mathrm{i}}+6 \mathrm{t}^2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}\right) \mathrm{N}$, where $\hat{\mathrm{i}}$ and $\hat{\mathrm{j}}$ are unit vectors along $x$ and y -axis respectively. The power produced by the force at $t=2 \mathrm{~s}$ is $\_\_\_\_$ W.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The velocity of a particle executing simple harmonic motion along $x$-axis is described as $v^2=50-x^2$, where $x$ represents displacement. If the time period of motion is $\frac{x}{7} \mathrm{~s}$, the value of $x$ is $\_\_\_\_$ .
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The two projectiles are projected with the same initial velocities at the $15^{\circ}$ and $30^{\circ}$ with respect to the horizontal. The ratio of their ranges is $1: x$. The value of $x$ is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A 1 kg block subjected to two simultaneous forces $(2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+4 \hat{\mathrm{k}}) \mathrm{N}$ and $(3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}) \mathrm{N}$ is moved a distance of 25 m along $(3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-4 \hat{\mathrm{j}})$ direction. The work done in this process is $\_\_\_\_$ J.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Match the List-I with List-II

List-I
Name of amino acid		List-II
One letter symbol/type
A. ArginineI. D/Non-essential
B. Aspartic acidII. R/Essential
C. LysineIII. E/Non-essential
D. Glutamic acidIV. K/Essential

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :

questions.questionText:
$$ \text { Match the LIST-I with LIST-II } $$

List-I
Complex ion		 List-II
Calculated spin only magnetic moment (BM)
A. $$
\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{2+}
$$		 I. 3.87
B. $$
\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{2+}
$$		 II. 5.92
C. $$
\left[\mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{2+}
$$		 III. 4.90
D. $$
\left[\mathrm{Mn}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{2+}
$$		 IV. 1.73

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options:
(A)

A-I, B-III, C-IV, D-II

(B)

A-II, B-I, C-III, D-IV

(C)

A-IV, B-II, C-I, D-III

(D)

A-III, B-I, C-IV, D-II

questions.questionText: Amongst the following, the total number of compounds soluble in aqueous NaOH at room temperature is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The surface tension of a soap solution is $3.5 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}$. The work required to increase the radius of a soap bubble from 1 cm to 2 cm is $\alpha \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~J}$. The value of $\alpha$ is $\_\_\_\_$ .

$$ (\pi=22 / 7) $$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : The structure of Maltose is given below:

Statement II : The structure of Lactose is given below :

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options:
(A)

Both Statement I and Statement II are true

(B)

Both Statement I and Statement II are false

(C)

Statement I is true but Statement II is false

(D)

Statement I is false but Statement II is true
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In order to oxidise a mixture of 1 mole each of $\mathrm{FeC}_2 \mathrm{O}_4, \mathrm{Fe}_2\left(\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4\right)_3, \mathrm{FeSO}_4$ and $\mathrm{Fe}_2\left(\mathrm{SO}_4\right)_3$ in acidic medium, the number of moles of $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ required is
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Product C of the following reaction sequence will be
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
$$ \text { Match the LIST-I with LIST-II } $$

$$
\begin{gathered}
\text { List-I } \\
\text { Name of reaction }
\end{gathered}
$$
 $$
\begin{gathered}
\text { List-II } \\
\text { Reagent or catalyst used }
\end{gathered}
$$
A. Finkelstein reaction I. $\mathrm{SbF}_3$
B. Swarts reaction II. Na, dry ether
C. Sandmeyer's reaction III. NaI
D. Fittig reaction IV. $\mathrm{Cu}_2 \mathrm{Cl}_2$

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :

questions.questionText:
The correct statements among the following are,

A. $\mathrm{Mo}(\mathrm{VI})$ and $\mathrm{W}(\mathrm{VI})$ are less stable than $\mathrm{Cr}(\mathrm{VI})$.

B. $\mathrm{Ce}^{4+}$ and $\mathrm{Tb}^{4+}$ are oxidant while $\mathrm{Eu}^{2+}$ and $\mathrm{Yb}^{2+}$ are reductant.

C. Cm and Am have seven unpaired electrons.

D. Actinoid contraction is greater from element to element than lanthanoid contraction.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options:
(A)

A and B Only

(B)

C and D Only

(C)

B and D Only

(D)

A and C Only
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
$$ \text { An alkene }(\mathrm{X}) \text { on ozonolysis followed by reduction gives following products. } $$

$$ \text { The alkene }(\mathrm{X}) \text { is : } $$
options: (A)
(B)
(C)
(D)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: What is the ratio of wave number of first line (lowest energy line) of Balmer series of H atomic spectrum to first line of its Brackett series?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Increasing order of electron withdrawing power of following functional groups is :

a. -CN

b. -COOH

c. $-\mathrm{NO}_2$

d. -I
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : The covalency of oxygen is generally two but it can exceed upto four. The oxidation state of oxygen in $\mathrm{SO}_2$ is -2 and in $\mathrm{OF}_2$ it is +2 .

Statement II : The anomalous behaviour of oxygen when compared to the other elements of group 16 is due to its small size and high electronegativity.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below
options:
(A)

Both Statement I and Statement II are true

(B)

Both Statement I and Statement II are false

(C)

Statement I is true but Statement II is false

(D)

Statement I is false but Statement II is true
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: $$ \text { Identify the colour of compound ' } \mathrm{X} \text { ' in the sequence of the reaction. } $$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : For an ideal gas, heat capacity at constant volume is always greater than the heat capacity at constant pressure.

Statement II : In a constant volume process, no work is produced and all the heat withdrawn goes into the chaotic motion and is reflected by a temperature increase of the ideal gas.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below
options:
(A)

Both Statement I and Statement II are true

(B)

Both Statement I and Statement II are false

(C)

Statement I is true but Statement II is false

(D)

Statement I is false but Statement II is true
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Number of moles and number of molecules in 1.4187 L of $\mathrm{SO}_2$ at STP respectively are
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
At $\mathrm{T}(\mathrm{K})$, the equilibrium constant of

$\mathrm{A}_2(g)+\mathrm{B}_2(g) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{C}(g)$ is $2.7 \times 10^{-5}$.

What is the equilibrium constant for

$\frac{1}{3} \mathrm{~A}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\frac{1}{3} \mathrm{~B}_2(\mathrm{~g}) \rightleftharpoons \frac{1}{3} \mathrm{C}(\mathrm{g})$ at the same temperature?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Consider the first order reaction $\mathrm{R} \rightarrow \mathrm{P}$.

The fraction of molecules decomposed in the given first order reaction can be expressed as
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A monoatomic anion (A ) has 45 neutrons and 36 electrons. Atomic mass, group in the periodic table and physical state at room temperature of the element (A) respectively are
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
$$ \text { Consider the following sequence of reactions to give the major product }(\mathrm{X}) $$

P g of the major product $(\mathrm{X})$ formed is reacted with $\mathrm{NaHCO}_3$ solution to liberate a gas which occupied $11.2 \mathrm{dm}^3$ at STP.

$$ \mathrm{P}=\text {______________ g. } $$

(Given molar mass in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1} \mathrm{H}: 1, \mathrm{C}: 12, \mathrm{O}: 16, \mathrm{Cl}: 35.5$ )
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
2.0 g of a bromo hydrocarbon $(\mathrm{X})$ was subjected to Carius analysis, gave 3.36 g of AgBr . The percentage of carbon in the compound $(\mathrm{X})$ is $26.7 \%$. Total number of carbon atoms in the empirical formula for compound $(\mathrm{X})$ is $\_\_\_\_$ .

(Given molar mass in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1} \mathrm{H}: 1, \mathrm{C}: 12, \mathrm{Br}: 80, \mathrm{Ag}: 108$ )
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The pH of a solution obtained by mixing 5 mL of $0.1 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}$ solution with 250 mL of $0.1 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}$ solution is $\_\_\_\_$ $\times 10^{-2}$.(Nearest integer) Given: $\mathrm{pK}_{\mathrm{b}}\left(\mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{OH}\right)=4.74$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \log 2=0.30 \\ & \log 3=0.48 \\ & \log 5=0.70 \end{aligned} $$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
A non-volatile, non-electrolyte solid solute when dissolved in 40 g of a solvent, the vapour pressure of the solvent decreased from 760 mm Hg to 750 mm Hg . If the same solution boils at 320 K , then the number of moles of the solvent present in the solution is $\_\_\_\_$ . (Nearest integer)

[Given: boiling point of the pure solvent $=319.5 \mathrm{~K}$,

$\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{b}}$ of the solvent $=0.3 \mathrm{~K} \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ ]
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which of the following is correct set of 4 quantum numbers of $19^{\text {th }}$ electron in Chromium (Atomic number $=24$ ) in accordance with Aufbau principle?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: According to Lewis theory, the total number of $\sigma$ bond-pairs and lone pair of electrons around the central atom of $\mathrm{XeO}_6{ }^{4-}$ ion is $\_\_\_\_$ .
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Correct statements from the following are

A. Potassium dichromate is an oxidising agent and it oxidises $\mathrm{FeSO}_4$ to $\mathrm{Fe}_2\left(\mathrm{SO}_4\right)_3$ in acidic medium.

B. Sodium dichromate can be used as primary standard in volumetric estimation.

C. $\mathrm{CrO}_4^{2-}$ and $\mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7^{2-}$ are interconvertible in aqueous solution by varying the pH of the solution.

D. $\mathrm{Cr}-\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{Cr}$ bond angle in $\mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7^{2-}$ is $126^{\circ}$.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options:
(A)

A, B and C Only

(B)

A, C and D Only

(C)

A and C Only

(D)

B and D Only
questions.subject: Chemistry
